joined_reference: it confirms fincher 's status as a film maker who artfully bends technical know-how to the service of psychological insight .  too much of it feels unfocused and underdeveloped .  a great ensemble cast ca n't lift this heartfelt enterprise out of the familiar .  prurient playthings aside , there 's little to love about this english trifle .  it moves quickly , adroitly , and without fuss ; it does n't give you time to reflect on the inanity -- and the cold war datedness -- of its premise .  it proves quite compelling as an intense , brooding character study .  bogdanovich tantalizes by offering a peep show into the lives of the era 's creme de la celluloid .  looks and feels like a project better suited for the small screen .  verbinski implements every hack-artist trick to give us the ooky-spookies .  this is a good script , good dialogue , funny even for adults .  a painfully funny ode to bad behavior .  there is a fabric of complex ideas here , and feelings that profoundly deepen them .  the overall effect is less like a children 's movie than a recruitment film for future hollywood sellouts .  as the two leads , lathan and diggs are charming and have chemistry both as friends and lovers .  the title not only describes its main characters , but the lazy people behind the camera as well .  zhang ... has done an amazing job of getting realistic performances from his mainly nonprofessional cast .

joined_reference: not only is undercover brother as funny , if not more so , than both austin powers films , but it 's also one of the smarter , savvier spoofs to come along in some time .  beautifully observed , miraculously unsentimental comedy-drama .  the last 20 minutes are somewhat redeeming , but most of the movie is the same teenage american road-trip drek we 've seen before - only this time you have to read the fart jokes  if you dig on david mamet 's mind tricks ... rent this movie and enjoy !  for all its impressive craftsmanship , and despite an overbearing series of third-act crescendos , lily chou-chou never really builds up a head of emotional steam .  yes , dull .  shaky close-ups of turkey-on-rolls , stubbly chins , liver spots , red noses and the filmmakers new bobbed do draw easy chuckles but lead nowhere .  the second coming of harry potter is a film far superior to its predecessor .  if you 're hard up for raunchy college humor , this is your ticket right here .  nonsensical , dull `` cyber-horror '' flick is a grim , hollow exercise in flat scares and bad acting .  a delectable and intriguing thriller filled with surprises , read my lips is an original .  thanks to scott 's charismatic roger and eisenberg 's sweet nephew , roger dodger is one of the most compelling variations on in the company of men .  there 's no emotional pulse to solaris .  a fast , funny , highly enjoyable movie .  too restrained to be a freak show , too mercenary and obvious to be cerebral , too dull and pretentious to be engaging ... the isle defies an easy categorization .  an unwise amalgam of broadcast news and vibes .

joined_reference: a pleasant enough romance with intellectual underpinnings , the kind of movie that entertains even as it turns maddeningly predictable .  it 's not original , and , robbed of the element of surprise , it does n't have any huge laughs in its story of irresponsible cops who love to play pranks .  it 's hard to imagine alan arkin being better than he is in this performance .  dazzles with its fully-written characters , its determined stylishness ( which always relates to characters and story ) and johnny dankworth 's best soundtrack in years .  fun , flip and terribly hip bit of cinematic entertainment .  the movie fails to live up to the sum of its parts .  one of the more intelligent children 's movies to hit theaters this year .  corpus collosum -- while undeniably interesting -- wore out its welcome well before the end credits rolled about 45 minutes in .  the performances take the movie to a higher level .  one of those pictures whose promising , if rather precious , premise is undercut by amateurish execution .  a compelling spanish film about the withering effects of jealousy in the life of a young monarch whose sexual passion for her husband becomes an obsession .  uses high comedy to evoke surprising poignance .  it has all the excitement of eating oatmeal .  feature debuter d.j. caruso directs a crack ensemble cast , bringing screenwriter tony gayton 's narcotics noir to life .  the old-world - meets-new mesh is incarnated in the movie 's soundtrack , a joyful effusion of disco bollywood that , by the end of monsoon wedding , sent my spirit soaring out of the theater .  an important movie , a reminder of the power of film to move us and to make us examine our values .

joined_reference: it 's a bit disappointing that it only manages to be decent instead of dead brilliant .  it 's not the ultimate depression-era gangster movie .  it has the charm of the original american road movies , feasting on the gorgeous , ramshackle landscape of the filmmaker 's motherland .  the vitality of the actors keeps the intensity of the film high , even as the strafings blend together .  the movie is n't just hilarious : it 's witty and inventive , too , and in hindsight , it is n't even all that dumb .  it 's difficult to imagine the process that produced such a script , but here 's guessing that spray cheese and underarm noises played a crucial role .  while locals will get a kick out of spotting cleveland sites , the rest of the world will enjoy a fast-paced comedy with quirks that might make the award-winning coen brothers envious .  broomfield turns his distinctive ` blundering ' style into something that could really help clear up the case .  oh come on .  awesome creatures , breathtaking scenery , and epic battle scenes add up to another ` spectacular spectacle . '  paid in full is so stale , in fact , that its most vibrant scene is one that uses clips from brian de palma 's scarface .  warm water under a red bridge is a quirky and poignant japanese film that explores the fascinating connections between women , water , nature , and sexuality .  the wild thornberrys movie is a jolly surprise .  the film flat lines when it should peak and is more missed opportunity and trifle than dark , decadent truffle .  for the first time in years , de niro digs deep emotionally , perhaps because he 's been stirred by the powerful work of his co-stars .  escaping the studio , piccoli is warmly affecting and so is this adroitly minimalist movie .

joined_reference: a moody , multi-dimensional love story and sci-fi mystery , solaris is a thought-provoking , haunting film that allows the seeds of the imagination to germinate .  a celebration of quirkiness , eccentricity , and certain individuals ' tendency to let it all hang out , and damn the consequences .  harris commands the screen , using his frailty to suggest the ravages of a life of corruption and ruthlessness .  it does nothing new with the old story , except to show fisticuffs in this sort of stop-go slow motion that makes the gang rumbles look like they 're being streamed over a 28k modem .  martin and barbara are complex characters -- sometimes tender , sometimes angry -- and the delicate performances by sven wollter and viveka seldahl make their hopes and frustrations vivid .  as a first-time director , paxton has tapped something in himself as an actor that provides frailty with its dark soul .  the result is a gaudy bag of stale candy , something from a halloween that died .  it is great summer fun to watch arnold and his buddy gerald bounce off a quirky cast of characters .  the best revenge may just be living well because this film , unlike other dumas adaptations , is far more likened to a treasure than a lengthy jail sentence .  sticky sweet sentimentality , clumsy plotting and a rosily myopic view of life in the wwii-era mississippi delta undermine this adaptation .  even if you do n't think ( kissinger 's ) any more guilty of criminal activity than most contemporary statesmen , he 'd sure make a courtroom trial great fun to watch .  forced , familiar and thoroughly condescending .  this is a winning ensemble comedy that shows canadians can put gentle laughs and equally gentle sentiments on the button , just as easily as their counterparts anywhere else in the world .  scores no points for originality , wit , or intelligence .  an entertaining , colorful , action-filled crime story with an intimate heart .  manages to be sweet and wickedly satisfying at the same time .
